zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de Bodrugan於1243年出生在英國可瓦爾時，她的父親Philip de Bodrugan19歲，母親Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion17歲。她於1259年與Sir Thomas de Pridias在英國可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane de Bodrugan于1243年出生在英国可瓦爾时，她的父亲Philip de Bodrugan19岁，母亲Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion17岁。她于1259年与Sir Thomas de Pridias在英国可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de Bodrugan was born in 1243, in Cornwall, England, her father, Philip de Bodrugan, was 19 and her mother, Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion, was 17. She married Sir Thomas de Pridias in 1259, in Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane de Bodrugan est née en 1243, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Philip de Bodrugan, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas de Pridias en 1259, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane de Bodrugan 1243 in Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Philip de Bodrugan 19 und ihre Mutter Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion 17. Sie hat 1259 in Cornwall, England Sir Thomas de Pridias geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Bodrugan è nata il nel 1243 nel Cornwall, England, sua padre, Philip de Bodrugan, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas de Pridias nel 1259, nel Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane de Bodruganが1243年にイギリスコルンワルで生まれた時，父Philip de Bodruganは19歳，母Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellionは17歳であった。1259年にイギリスコルンワルでSir Thomas de Pridiasと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de Bodrugan(이)가 1243년 영국 코른월에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philip de Bodrugan(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1259년 영국 코른월에서 Sir Thomas de Pridias(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas de Pridias em 1259, em Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de Bodrugan родилась в 1243 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Philip de Bodrugan было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Roselyn de Hendresuk - Heiress of Endellion, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas de Pridias в 1259 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.
